>survivorship bias as variations and lvl 7/8/9 comps are all included in the overall stats. This also allows us to detect high performing, off-meta and upcoming builds faster and more accurately than manually maintained tier lists.
Q: Why do the option placements differ from the main comp, even if they have the same units?
A: The stats displayed on the main comp represent all variations of that composition. The options stats are only for that exact set of units, and don
Assign to unit. Type to search units
Stats on the best tft champions to play in set {{set}}. Select a unit to see the best items and builds for that unit. Data updates every 5 minutes.
Track unit performance over time by {{metrics}}. Filter by cost, star level, and number of items.
Track item performance over time by {{metrics}}. Filter by item type including Normal, Radiant, Artifact, Emblem, and Trait items.
Stats on the best {{itemType}} to build in TFT Set {{set}}. Select an item to see more stats on that item, such as best item holders and performance by stage. Data updates every 5 minutes.
Stats on how the item {{itemName}} performs in the current TFT meta. Find performance by stage and the best units to put {{itemName}} on. Data updates every 5 minutes
Number of items on this unit
TFT {{traitName}} Trait - Stats, Units and Best Comps
Stats on how {{traitName}} performs in the current TFT Set {{setNum}} meta. See the best Units, Trait Synergies, and top Comps featuring {{traitName}}. Data updates every 5 minutes.
Click a Trait to Filter Stats
Click a Unit to Filter Stats
Stats on the best traits to play in the current meta. Data updates every 5 minutes.
Find the most frequent Units and Unit star levels that go with your filters
Find the best Items and Item Holders that go with your filters
Find the best builds for units matching your filters
Find the best Additional Traits that go with your filters - Calculated via Trait Differences (includes Emblems on Golems, Training Dummies, and Units
